titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Marie Borroff Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Camila Zorrilla Tessler

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday July 1, 2023 at 07:36

did.abstract: Marie Borroff (1923-2019) was a translator, scholar, poet, and professor at Yale University. The collection contains Borroff's research and teaching files, correspondence, poetry, and other writings, including Borroff's work in computer poetry.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains research and teaching files, correspondence, poetry, and other writings of Marie Borroff, a professor in the Yale University department of English. A bulk of the material in this collection consists of Borroff's teaching notes and files, particularly on the topic of the Sir Gawain poems, Chaucer, and Middle English. Also included is correspondence with other scholars on topics such as academia, poetry, and translation.

Also included is Borroff's collaboration with Jay (Jack) Rawlins, Borroff's former student, who went on to teach at California's Chico State College. This project, which ran from 1977-1980, had the end goal of writing a textbook on poetry. It was eventually dropped due to incompatible writing styles.

Borroff's experimental computer poetry, part of her Guggenheim Fellowship project, is also represented in the collection. The project involved devising programs for writing poetry by computer, using a vocabulary acquired at random by closing her eyes, opening books of poetry, and putting a finger down on the page to find the next qualified word. Borroff also used a random number generator provided by the Yale Computer Center to write "frames" with blanks that were then filled in with appropriate words. The collection contains the poetry, correspondence that refers to the project, and programming materials.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Marie Borroff (1923-2019) was a translator, scholar, poet, and professor at Yale University. Borroff was born in New York City and attended the University of Chicago, where she received both her bachelor and master of arts degrees. She then attended Yale and received a PhD in English literature and philology.

Boroff began her academic career teaching at Smith College, then returned to Yale in 1959. She was the first woman appointed as faculty to the department of English. She gained tenure in 1965, making her the first of two women granted tenure in any department of Yale's Faculty of Arts and Sciences. During this time Borroff also produced critical studies on Sir Gawain and the Green Knight and new translations of the Gawain poems. Her translations were printed by the Norton Anthology.

In 1969, Borroff received a Guggenheim Fellowship and spent the year engaged in experimental poetry, particularly "computer poetry." The project was an early use of computer programming to generate poetry. Between 1977 and 1980, Borroff collaborated with Jay Rawlins on a textbook on poetry; it was ultimately not published.
In 1991, Borroff became the first woman to be named Sterling Professor, the highest academic honor awarded at Yale University. She retired from teaching in 1994.
Borroff died on July 5, 2019, at age 95.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site. Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright and other associated proprietary rights for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Marie Borroff was transferred to Yale University in 2020. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact beinecke.library@yale.edu.

Copyright status for Marie Moments Volume II: Summer Scrapbooks by Beatrice Bartlett, Box 26, Folder 25 and other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Steven M. Shepard and Margarett Jolly, 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged in five series: I. Research files, 1920-2018. II. Yale University, 1931-2019. III. Writings, 1916-2012. IV. Correspondence, 1912-2014. V. Family files, 1883-2019.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Marie Borroff papers (MS 2097). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

subject.part: Sir Gawain and the Green Knight

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged under three headings: Early English language, Computer poetry, and Poetry analyses. Materials within these groupings are arranged as received from the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains files pertaining to academic research conducted by Borroff and includes copies of articles, papers, and collaborations with other researchers. Also included are materials pertaining to Borroff's computer poetry project, including research done to create the computer programs, correspondence, and the computer poetry. Also contained is Borroff's collaboration with Jack Rawlins regarding computer poetry and textbook authorship.

did.unittitle: The Style of Gawain and the Green Knight thesis
Statistical approach of the question of common authorship
"If Wern Fore Letterys of Purposy" a new interpretation
How Hard is Middle English? Handout
Excerpts from Gawain and the Green Knight handouts
Papers on old and advanced old English
Enclosure and inclusion in Sir Gawain and the Green Knight
De Tribus Regibus Mortuis: four foes of mankind
Essay in Studies in the Age of Chaucer
David Ferry's translation of Abraham and Isaac
Bloom chapbook "A Whisper of Belatedness in Robert Frost"
Language of Chaucer's poetry class material
Style and language in Chaucer: an exercise
Chaucer courses: descriptions, bibliographies, syllabi, reserve list
Chaucer and the Woman from Bath
Middle English Dictionary, entries relevant to Chaucer
Published essay on the Minor Poems and Troilus
Tag values in Chaucer's language of sexuality
Pamphlet gathered by Richard W. Bailey
Anthology with explanatory preface and technical notes
The Poet, the Computer, and the Classroom
Creativity, Poetic Language, and the Computer
How the Home Computer Can Be Domesticated article
In The Tinfoil of the Moon…the Roses Wink
Random Stanza 4 (first trial, with inflectional errors)
History of the English language: a collection of introductory essays, exericses, and historical text
History of the English language, volume II
Old English chronicle: texts, commentary, and exercises
Erato, for generating lyric poems from first lines of modern poets
Yale computer center operating system guide
Creativity, poetic language, and the computer
Talks and essays on poetry and gender
Highlights of sense in Sir Gawain and the Green Knight
Sound symbolism as drama in the poetry of Wallace Stevens
Questions of design in Marianne Moore and William Carlos William
Sound symbolism as drama in Frost: PMLA journal, longer drafts, correspondence
Experimental approaches to sound symbolism articles
Off prints: talk to Ezra Stiles seniors
Finding the poem in the words - the club
Cluster on the poetic: From Euripides to Rich
William Carlos Williams: The Diagnostic Eye
Teaching in the Humanities, Humanities advisory committee
Visual vs. aural aspects of poetry
John Crowne Ransom and Louis MacNeice
Informal speech, Edward C. Caterette and Margaret Hubbard Jones
Marianne Moore, Edward Thomas, James Dickey poems
E. J. Dobson "Early Modern Standard English"
A poem in modern Scots dialect by Hugh M'Diarmid
Words and sounds Gawain poet Wallace Steven
Talk "The Auroras of Autumn" in Langdon Hammer's course
Materials on Ezra Pound lecture and University of North Carolina - Asheville

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized under two headings: Courses and teaching and Administrative. Materials within these groupings are arranged as received from the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Yale series contains class assignments, exams, class notes, research, committee notes, award notification, and outreach work relating to Borroff's professional career at Yale. This series does not contain student work.

did.unittitle: English 390, History of the English Language from the Beginning to 1500
Considering our language preliminaries to the study of its history
Course readings, History of the English Language to 1500
Change and growth in language: metaphor and metonymy
Treatment of loan words, early and Middle English romance doublets
Syntax, parts of speech, statue dynamic
History of the English Language, Chaucer to Shakespeare
John Ho Fisher, Chancery and the emergence of standard written English in the 15th century
John Trevisa's translation of Higden's Polychronicon
Selections from the prose writings of William Caxton
Romance and Latinate loan words rules of thumb
Maps showing the distribution of three minor forms in late Middle English
History of English: 1500 to present notecards
History of English: 1500 to present notecards
History of English: 1500 to present, 20th century poetry, Chaucer and vocabulary notes
Old English and old Norse cognates
Old English prose passages other than Psalms and Genesis
Old English poetry, Fred Robinson on Stangiella
Old English normalized Genesis 1-2, psalms, editions, facsimile
History of French: Little Gaulish and no English
Essay on the minstrel's grave, translation by Steve Davis
History of English syllabus, reserve lists, registration materials
History of English, the history of the alphabet
Expressive values of words, terms; metaphor/metonymy
Essay for the Yale Review on prescriptive grammar: "Fowler and the Rest"
Thomas Nashe or William Painter Arcadia North
William Caxton translation of Thomas Malory
Another look at Robert Frost's "Birches" article
Centenary edition, the complete works of Ralph Waldo Emerson
Canon, gender, and text: the case of Hilda Dolittle
Possible article on medical jargon for transactions and studies
Harry Truman and Douglas MacArthur speeches
Examples of language/stylistic/phenomenons/topics in language notecards
Marie Borroff, Final Lecture, April 22, 1993 (On her own poetry)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site. Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository.

did.unittitle: Faculty of Arts and Sciences Advisory Committee on the Education of Women at Yale: Report correspondence and response summary
Faculty of Arts and Sciences Advisory Committee on the Education of Women: Tenuring of women at Yale report
Faculty of Arts and Sciences Advisory Committee on the Education of Women report
Koerner center advisory board meeting minutes
Elizabethan Club medal for distinguished service

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is arranged under two headings: Published and Unpublished. Materials within these groupings are arranged as received from the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains material written by Borroff. Published material refers to any material that were published as of Borroff's death in 2019, including journals, poems, or presentations. Unpublished material is largely drafts of Borroff's writing, but also includes a scrapbook assembled by Beatrice Bartlett on the topic of Borroff's life.

did.unittitle: I was wondering what you looked like
John Collins Pope: a friendship of the mind
Guidelines for Equal Treatment of the Sexes in Advertising layouts
John Collins Pope: a memorial tribute
Shakespeare's Trojan Queens and Tragic Sympathies
Rafting Down the Grand Canyon: A Meditation
Stars and Other Signs and Steer, earlier poems
Light Verse by Marie Borroff and others
New Poems and revisions of old poems
Good Gouge: an investigation into the origins of Naval Academy Slang
Journal of Medieval and Renaissance studies volume 3, number 1
Palaeography and Poetry: Some Solved and Unsolved Problems of the Exeter Book
Medieval scholarship: biographical studies on the formation of a discipline, 2, literature and philology
Grouped frequency word-list of Anglo-Saxon poetry
On the date of composition of Beowulf
Anti-Semitism and the English mystery plays
Sword Hrunting in Beowulf: unlocking the word Word
Nomination and the Dynamics of the Clerk's Tale: Homo Viator as Woman
Old English Maria: edited with introduction and notes by J.M. Pritzen
New kodak moment with Emily Dickinson
Poems by Marie Borroff: a selection in chronological order
Mary to Joseph, Christ I, 164-67a, a probable scribal error nu for na
What the Seraphim do in line 396a of the Old English Advent: a scribal cover uncovered
Marie moments, volume II: Summer scrapbook by Beatrice Bartlett

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains primarily Borroff's personal correspondence that documents her friendships. The professional correspondence in this series largely consists of congratulatory notes for promotions, or letters of inquiry for collaboration or written material. Professional correspondence that specifically addresses other topics that are covered in this collection is filed with that material to reflect the creator's original order.

did.unittitle: Blum, John M. correspondence and memorabilia
Letters from friends, editors, etc about Borroff's poetry
Thank you notes and memorial correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series V is arranged as recieved from the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of Borroff's family materials, including travel documents, journals, correspondence, photographs, and family history.

did.unittitle: Louise Cushman, Virginia and Lorrin Shepherd correspondence
Marie Borroff's Friends from Pinewoods' Early Music Week Celebrate Her 95th Birthday
Sketchbooks, "Jane Juniper," Funnies by Marie Borroff
"Marie's letters from Mrs. Mc's Dormitory, Chicago, Illinois," Winter 1940-1941
